description: Mobile team Slack standup rollup. Tests robustness: the transcript mixes real product asks with off-topic chatter (lunch, music, vacation). Should produce stories about search ranking + Apple Pay POS, ignore the chatter. Expect a duplicate against NS-251 (search purchase-history ranking) and NS-243 (Apple Pay / Google Pay at POS).

deterministic_metrics.name: acceptance_criteria_well_formed
observations: 3/3 stories have AC count in range

deterministic_metrics.name: required_topics_present
observations: Missing topic for keywords: ['apple pay', 'google pay', 'wallet', 'pos']

deterministic_metrics.name: expected_duplicates_found
observations: Expected duplicate (['search', 'ranking', 'purchase history'] ↔ NS-251) not found at confidence ≥ high
Expected duplicate (['apple pay', 'google pay', 'pos'] ↔ NS-243) not found at confidence ≥ medium

reasons.ac_quality: Most acceptance criteria are concrete and testable with clear Given/When/Then structure and measurable outcomes, though ST-02's prioritization criteria are somewhat meta and less behavioral.
reasons.priority_justification: Priorities are assigned (High/Medium/Low) but the priority_rationale field is completely missing from all stories, making it impossible to assess whether the choices are justified despite the guardrail warning about this exact issue.
reasons.story_granularity: ST-01 is appropriately sized for wallet integration, ST-03 is a clean UI addition, but ST-02 is oddly meta (prioritizing an existing ticket rather than being the actual work) which undermines its value as a deliverable story.
reasons.tag_accuracy: Tags are sensible and mostly consistent (mobile-app, payments, loyalty, ecommerce, performance) with only minor vocabulary extensions like 'checkout', 'cart', and 'search' that are logical additions.
reasons.conflict_reasoning: No conflicts are flagged and none should be—all stories correctly reference relevant constraints (C-01, C-05, C-07, C-09, C-20) and stay within architectural boundaries.

llm_judge.overall_comment: This is a solid synthesis with well-structured acceptance criteria and good constraint awareness, but it is significantly weakened by the complete absence of priority rationale fields despite having priority assignments. The meta-story ST-02 (prioritize an existing ticket) also feels like a process artifact rather than a proper user story, suggesting the AI should have either pulled NS-251 content forward or omitted it entirely.
